key_points: Pressure is the force acting on a unit area of a surface
When the area of contact decreases, pressure increases
Air around us exerts pressure called atmospheric pressure
Wind is the movement of air from a region of high pressure to a region of low pressure
Storms are strong disturbances in the atmosphere with powerful winds
Cyclones are large rotating storms that form over warm ocean waters
examples: Sharp knives cut easily because they apply greater pressure
Camels have broad feet to reduce pressure on sand
Tractors have wide tyres to prevent sinking in soil
Cyclones bring heavy rain and strong winds to coastal regions
formulas: Wind movement: Air flows from high pressure to low pressure
exam_tips: Remember the formula and unit of pressure
Understand how pressure changes with area
Learn examples showing practical use of pressure
Know basic causes and effects of cyclones and storms
